summary: Small cleanup PR for Pokémon Stadium stage code. It replaced an inline anonymous rules struct cast in grStadium_801D3460 with the named StartMeleeRules type returned by gm_8016AE50, replaced a magic player-slot constant with Gm_PKind_NA, and corrected gm_8016AEEC's return type from bool to int to reflect that it returns timer_seconds.
what_changed: In src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.c, changed gm_8016AEEC from returning bool to returning int while still returning lbl_8046B6A0.timer_seconds.
In src/melee/gm/gm_16AE.h, updated the gm_8016AEEC prototype to match the implementation return type int.
In src/melee/gr/grstadium.c, replaced a local anonymous bitfield struct pointer temp_r3_4 with StartMeleeRules* rules.
In grStadium_801D3460, replaced the casted gm_8016AE50 result with a typed assignment: rules = gm_8016AE50().
In grStadium_801D3460, changed Player_GetPlayerSlotType(i) != 3 to Player_GetPlayerSlotType(i) != Gm_PKind_NA.
The existing conditional using rules data was preserved semantically: (rules->x10 == 0) | !rules->x0_6.
smart_moves: Used the named StartMeleeRules type instead of keeping a one-off anonymous layout in stage code, making the dependency on match rules explicit and reusable.
Removed a magic numeric slot-type comparison and replaced it with Gm_PKind_NA, improving readability and reducing risk of future enum-value mistakes.
Corrected gm_8016AEEC's return type to match the value being returned, timer_seconds, rather than treating it as a boolean.
Kept the grStadium conditional structure unchanged while improving its types, minimizing matching risk during cleanup.
decomp_lessons: When a function returns a field such as timer_seconds, avoid bool unless the value is truly a predicate; use an integer type consistent with the field's semantics.
Anonymous local struct overlays are useful during early matching, but should be replaced with project structs such as StartMeleeRules once the layout is known.
Replace raw enum constants such as 3 with named constants such as Gm_PKind_NA as soon as the enum meaning is known.
Stage files may depend on game-mode rule structures; typing those accesses can clean up temporary casts without changing behavior.
Small header corrections matter because they propagate better types to call sites and reduce the need for casts or fake local structs.
naming_conventions: Use StartMeleeRules for the object returned by gm_8016AE50 when accessing rule fields such as x10 and x0_6.
Use Gm_PKind_NA instead of the literal player-kind value 3.
Name a StartMeleeRules pointer rules rather than keeping compiler-temp style names like temp_r3_4 when the semantic role is known.
Keep existing unresolved gm_16AE function names such as gm_8016AEEC when no stronger symbol name is evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: A cleanup can improve source types while preserving code shape; the PR changed temp_r3_4->x10 and temp_r3_4->x0_6 to rules->x10 and rules->x0_6 without altering the conditional expression.
Changing a return type from bool to int can be a low-risk match/readability fix when the returned field is already integer-like and no body logic changes are required.
Replacing a magic constant with an enum member can preserve generated behavior if the enum value is equivalent.
follow_up_queries: Find other callers of gm_8016AEEC that may still treat timer_seconds as bool.
Search for remaining Player_GetPlayerSlotType comparisons against raw constants instead of Gm_PKind_* enum names.
Search for remaining anonymous structs or casts around gm_8016AE50 that should use StartMeleeRules.
Review StartMeleeRules field names x0_6 and x10 for future semantic naming once their rule meanings are known.
